experiment: Global RG-flow minimization test (strong LVS form)
hypothesis: SM parameters (especially alpha_s) minimize the RG-flow action S_LVS = integral of sum_i beta_i^2 from M_Z to M_Pl
method: Integrate 1-loop SM RGEs for (g1, g2, g3, y_t) from M_Z=172.76 GeV to M_Pl=1.22e19 GeV, scan alpha_s(M_Z) in [0.01, 0.16], compute action and Euclidean length for each.
notes: Measured alpha_s sits at ~2.4x the optimum. For alpha_s < 0.04 the top Yukawa hits a Landau pole before M_Pl, so the identified minimum is not a boundary artifact. This definitively rules out the strong LVS formulation as a global variational principle over the SM RG flow. The weaker Planck-BC form still survives - see eichhorn_held_comparison.json.
